name: United Arab Emirates
summary: Day 67 Tuesday. **UAE FUJAIRAH OIL INDUSTRY ZONE STRUCK BY IRANIAN DRONE MAY 4** — first Iranian attack on UAE since the Apr 8 ceasefire. Three Indian nationals moderately injured. Four cruise missiles also launched at UAE — three intercepted by UAE MoD over territorial waters, fourth crashed into sea. Fujairah Oil Industry Zone sits at the END of the Abu Dhabi Crude Oil Pipeline that BYPASSES Hormuz — the asset Iran chose to strike was specifically the UAE's bypass infrastructure. UAE airspace partially closed May 4-11 (BusinessToday). Tasnim threatened 'if the UAE takes unwise action, all of its interests will become Iran's target.' The May 4 attack happened during Day 7-of-14 watching window for Saudi MBS direct response on UAE OPEC exit (effective May 1) — MBS still NO direct statement. Saudi former oil adviser al-Sabban dismissed UAE OPEC exit publicly ('not a major blow') but no royal-court response. Chatham House published 'How the Iran war is reshaping Saudi strategy' framing UAE OPEC exit as confirmed Saudi setback. Atlantic Council 'A long time coming' frames structural inevitability. The UAE: chose to leave OPEC just as Iran chose to make UAE a kinetic target — sequencing isn't coincidence. UAE strategic position dramatically more exposed than 7 days ago: outside cartel umbrella + direct kinetic exposure + Indian-citizen casualty triggering New Delhi MEA 'unacceptable' statement.

active_triggers.condition: UAE enters war as combatant (not just defensive)
UAE adds 500K-1M bpd of supply within 6 months of OPEC exit (May 1) confirming Atlantic Council/Derentz medium-term-bearish thesis
Saudi Arabia issues formal public response to UAE OPEC exit within 14 days (by 2026-05-12)

historical_summaries.summary: 1 signals. Iran struck US Consulate in Dubai, Dubai International Airport, and Fairmont The Palm hotel in initial retaliation wave

5 signals. ADNOC Ruwais refinery (UAE's largest, 922K bpd capacity) shut down after Iranian drone strike caused fire | Iran struck Shah gas field (set ablaze) and Fujairah port (key oil hub used as Hormuz bypass) | IRGC threatened US university campuses in Gulf on March 29, specifically naming NYU Abu Dhabi

15 signals. UAE described as 'perhaps the most hawkish' among Gulf allies, pushing for ground invasion of Iran (WaPo national security desk) | Al-Salmi VLCC (Kuwait-flagged, 2M bbl Saudi/Kuwaiti oil for China) struck at Dubai port anchorage March 31 — most significant single-vessel | CONFIRMED: Borouge petrochemical plant ($60B, world's largest single-site polyolefin plant) SHUT DOWN operations at Ruwais Industrial Comple
